player_notes.jalen brunson: Dropped 38 points in Game 1 OT win vs. Cavs, sparking a 22-point 4th-quarter comeback. High confidence in points and assists props heading into Game 2.
player_notes.james harden: Was relentlessly targeted and exploited by Brunson in Game 1; poor defensive performance may signal reduced effectiveness on both ends. Monitor usage if Cavs adjust.
player_notes.shai gilgeous-alexander: Self-admitted subpar Game 1 vs. Spurs despite winning MVP; expect bounce-back attempt in Game 2, but Spurs defense held him in check.
player_notes.chet holmgren: Criticized after being outplayed by Wembanyama in Game 1; potential confidence/minutes concern if Thunder make lineup/role adjustments for Game 2.
player_notes.victor wembanyama: Dominant Game 1 performance vs. Thunder; points, rebounds, blocks props have elevated floor with Spurs holding 1-0 series lead.
player_notes.dylan harper: Electric Game 1 performance for Spurs vs. Thunder; emerging role warrants attention on points and assists props for Game 2.

game_notes.SAS vs OKC: Spurs lead 1-0; Wembanyama dominant, SGA underperformed. Thunder may adjust rotation/scheme, watch for minutes changes around Holmgren.
game_notes.NYK vs CLE: Knicks lead 1-0 after historic comeback; Cavs coach Atkinson under scrutiny for late-game management. Cavs may adjust Harden's role defensively in Game 2.
